channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Argentina vs Switzerland: World Cup quarterfinal – Messi, prediction, news
item.description: Lionel Messi's Argentina faces Breel Embolo's Switzerland for a place in the semifinals of the FIFA World Cup 2026.

item.title: Venezuelan doctors convert restaurant into a clinic after earthquake
item.description: Volunteers in La Guaira have transformed a fast-food restaurant into an emergency clinic.

item.title: Meta backtracks on AI-image feature for Instagram due to privacy backlash
item.description: Meta has rolled back its ‘Muse Image’ AI feature after widespread backlash over privacy and consent.

item.title: Scaloni not surprised by Messi’s World Cup form at 39 – ‘He’s a machine’
item.description: A muscle strain threatened to derail Messi's tournament, but his coach says he's still world's best player.

item.title: Are the US and Iran at war again?
item.description: Iranian leaders say they’ll never surrender and US President Donald Trump says the ceasefire is ‘over'.

item.title: ‘We are the lost generation of Sudan’: Students in exile
item.description: Years of conflict have left thousands of Sudanese students struggling to continue their education in exile.

item.title: McGregor makes weight ahead of UFC 329 bout against Holloway
item.description: Conor McGregor, former two-division champion and still one of UFC's most famous fighters, has not fought since 2021.

item.title: World Cup: Kane vs Haaland in England-Norway quarterfinal striker battle
item.description: Haaland termed a 'beast' by England's Kane as the lethal forwards aim to carry their teams into the semifinals.

item.title: Yamal does not mind lack of goals if Spain win World Cup 2026
item.description: Spanish star has scored just one goal in the World Cup, where he has been sharing celebrations with his little brother.

item.title: Zaporizhzhia’s mayor says Russian advance reaches city’s outskirts
item.description: Emergency crews are searching for survivors after a Russian air strike on the Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia.

item.title: Why the new US housing bill won’t fix the crisis
item.description: Why the new US housing bill won't fix the crisis.

item.title: Iran war live: Trump threatens to ‘decimate’ Iran if it tries to kill him
item.description: US president says orders given to 'decimate and destroy all areas of Iran' if he is assassinated

item.title: Nicaragua strips lawyers of certification in latest crackdown on dissent
item.description: The government of Daniel Ortega and Rosario Murillo has been accused of human rights abuses against critics.

item.title: Cuba suffers second island-wide blackout in a week amid Trump fuel blockade
item.description: US President Donald Trump has placed the Caribbean island under a de facto oil blockade, straining its power grid.

item.title: Apple files lawsuit accusing ChatGPT maker OpenAI of stealing trade secrets
item.description: The complaint alleges a coordinated effort by two former Apple employees and OpenAI to steal confidential information.

item.title: Colombia’s Jaminton Campaz receives death threats after World Cup exit
item.description: Campaz was threatened after failing to score in the last-16 match against Switzerland, which Colombia lost on penalties.

item.title: Under Lula, Amazon deforestation falls to lowest level in a decade
item.description: The figures come months before Brazil’s presidential election, with Lula promising to end illegal deforestation by 2030.

item.title: Canada’s Bill C-36 tackles AI privacy. Is it enough?
item.description: Canada’s new privacy bill promises stronger protections for children, but experts say it still misses risks in the AI world.

item.title: Spain leave it late to book semifinal date with France
item.description: Mikel Merino comes off the bench to break Belgian hearts after slip from sub keeper Senne Lammens.

item.title: Dozens of abducted schoolchildren and teachers rescued in Nigeria
item.description: The hostages were taken from three separate schools in the southwestern state of Oyo in May.

item.title: Lebanon historic sites destroyed by Israeli strikes
item.description: Israeli air strikes on southern Lebanon have caused catastrophic destruction and damaged historic landmarks.

item.title: How Wong Kim Ark’s legacy reignited the fight for birthright citizenship
item.description: Sandra Wong did not know her great-grandfather growing up. But his legacy would catapult her into the public eye.

item.title: Ukrainian attacks cause chaos at fuel stations across Russia
item.description: Ukraine’s targeting of Russia’s major oil refineries has sparked a nationwide fuel shortage.

item.title: Donald Trump removes final members of independent US election commission
item.description: The dismissals leave the federal election body vacant as Trump presses for broader changes to US voting rules.

item.title: Egypt World Cup team return home to hero’s welcome after historic run
item.description: Egypt’s squad has returned home to a hero’s welcome following their controversial exit from the World Cup.
